title: Phase 2 — Where the Revenue Comes From
subtitle: The four monetization lines that turn Phase 1's logged history into a business
description: Premium is not one price — it is a subscription plus a take-rate plus paid services. This chart names the four revenue lines and, deliberately, leaves every figure empty: none has a measured price point, attach rate, or willingness-to-pay yet. It is rendered as stages rather than a pie precisely because a pie would require percentages that would have to be fabricated.
SYNTHETIC_WARNING: No revenue figure here is measured. Each line's contribution is unknown until Phase 2 has real paying customers. Rendered empty on purpose.

data.required_input: Measured willingness-to-pay and free-to-paid conversion — awaiting Phase 2
data.note: Predictive health alerts, analytics, and recommendations trained on the farmer's own logged history.

data.required_input: Marketplace GMV and take-rate — no listings or orders exist yet
data.note: Feed, equipment, livestock, and buyer matching. Paid by design because a third party is also getting paid.

data.required_input: Consultation booking rate and fee — awaiting Phase 2
data.note: Paid access to vets and extension officers, routed from the disease-ID and assistant flows.

data.required_input: Measured hardware-service price and unit economics — Phase 3 is TRL 3, nothing flown
data.note: The eventual deepest revenue line, sold as an upgrade. Shown here to place it on the same spine, not to claim it is near-term.

rendering_guidance: Render every value cell EMPTY with the required-input filled. This is a modeled structure of revenue lines, not a forecast of their sizes.
